title: Assistant Finance Manager
description:
Assistant Finance Manager

Key Responsibilities

  • Prepare timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements.

  • Ensure compliance with statutory reporting requirements and accounting standards (IFRS/GAAP).

  • Compile supporting schedules and liaise with auditors and tax agents for audits, filings, and submissions.

  • Manage the full set of accounts, including Accounts Receivable (AR), Accounts Payable (AP), and General Ledger (GL).

  • Handle bank postings, reconciliations, payments, journal entries, and maintenance of financial schedules.

  • Administer GST filings, submissions, and claims in accordance with Singapore regulations.

  • Monitor cash flow and working capital to ensure liquidity and financial stability.

  • Assess foreign exchange exposure and support hedging strategies where required.

  • Develop and manage annual budgets, forecasts, and cash flow projections.

  • Track actual performance against budget and prepare variance analysis with actionable insights.

  • Provide financial analysis and recommendations to support management decision-making.

  • Implement, maintain, and enhance internal controls, policies, and procedures.

  • Ensure compliance with tax laws, corporate governance, and regulatory requirements.

  • Coordinate internal and external audits, regulatory reviews, and compliance matters.

  • Set KPIs for the finance team and ensure timely and accurate delivery of outputs.

  • Lead, coach, and develop finance team members, assigning tasks and monitoring progress.

  • Promote a culture of continuous improvement, accountability, and results orientation.

  • Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to streamline workflows, strengthen controls, and identify cost efficiencies.

  • Support ad-hoc finance duties and special projects as required.

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline.

  • CPA / ACCA or equivalent professional qualification preferred.

  • Minimum 5 years of relevant accounting or finance experience, ideally within the marine, offshore, or engineering industry.

  • Strong working knowledge of Singapore financial regulations, GST, and tax laws.

  • Proficient in ERP accounting systems; SAP experience is an advantage.

  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

  • Able to work independently while effectively managing team and stakeholder expectations.

postedCompany.description:
At Direct Search Asia we deliver innovative and flexible recruitment solutions for partners primarily within the shipping, marine & offshore, oil & gas and logistics sectors. We treat businesses and individuals equally - sharing information, challenging assumptions, maintaining open communications and operating with integrity.

Wherever you plan for your career or business to be, we have a plan to help get you there.

Visit us at www.directsearchasia.com/vacancies and follow us on LinkedIn for more information and opportunities
